content.problemStatement:
Chef has a tasty ingredient ― an integer KKK. He defines a tasty matrix AAA with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM columns (numbered 111 through MMM) as Ai,j=K+i+jA_{i, j} = K + i + jAi,j​=K+i+j for each valid i,ji,ji,j.

Currently, Chef is busy in the kitchen making this tasty matrix. Help him find the bitwise XOR of all elements of this matrix.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1062 \cdot 10^62⋅106
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1062 \cdot 10^62⋅106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the bitwise XOR of all elements of the tasty matrix with the given dimensions made with the given special ingredient.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Example case 1: The matrix is 5 + 1 + 1 & 5 + 1 + 2\\ 5 + 2 + 1 & 5 + 2 + 2\end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 7 & 8 \\ 8 & 9 \end{pmatrix} \,.$$ The XOR of all its elements is $7 \oplus 8 \oplus 8 \oplus 9 = 14$. **Example case 2:** The matrix is $$A = \begin{pmatrix} 7 + 1 + 1 & 7 + 1 + 2 & 7 + 1 + 3\\ 7 + 2 + 1 & 7 + 2 + 2 & 7 + 2 + 3 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 9 & 10 & 11\\ 10 & 11 & 12 \end{pmatrix} \,.$$ The XOR of all its elements is $9 \oplus 10 \oplus 11 \oplus 10 \oplus 11 \oplus 12 = 5$.
